# Prole

> Prole is a free, native macOS menu-bar app that captures any region of your screen, lets you annotate it (arrows, pen, and a text tool that writes right on the snip), then copies the annotated image for you to paste into Claude — or anywhere you can paste an image. It is a visual "ask about my screen" tool — pixels and your notes only, no DOM or code extraction. Claude is the flagship use case, but the copied image is a standard screenshot that works in any app.

Prole runs on macOS 12 or later (universal — Apple Silicon and Intel). It is ad-hoc signed, so first launch needs a right-click → Open. Download: https://prole.jeffrey.build/download/Prole.dmg

## How it works

1. Trigger a snip from the menu bar, a floating button, or a global hotkey, then draw a rectangle around any region of any app.
2. Annotate it with rectangles, arrows, and freehand pen, and use the text tool to write your question directly on the snip.
3. Hit Copy — the annotated image lands on your clipboard. Paste it into Claude to ask — or into ChatGPT, Slack, email, a doc, or any app that accepts a pasted image.

## Key facts

- Price: free
- Platform: macOS 12+ (universal: Apple Silicon and Intel)
- Distribution: ad-hoc signed .dmg (right-click → Open on first launch; grant Screen Recording permission on first capture)
- What it captures: screen pixels plus any notes you annotate onto the image (no source code, selectors, or DOM)
- Made for pasting into: Claude (flagship use case), or any app that accepts a pasted image (ChatGPT, Slack, email, docs, etc.)

## Links

- Home: https://prole.jeffrey.build/
- Download (macOS .dmg): https://prole.jeffrey.build/download/Prole.dmg
- Source: https://github.com/BadMask121/ai-agents
